meridian_skill.description: Compile daily briefing with meeting context, active deals, and citation tracking
meridian_skill.name: briefing

sections.heading: Contract
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Phases
lines: codeblock:bash:Z2JyYWluIHJlY2FsbCAtLXNpbmNlLWxhc3QtcnVuIC0tc3VwZXJzZXNzaW9ucyAtLXBlbmRpbmcgLS1yb2xsdXAgLS1qc29u
use judgment to compose the daily briefing:
Fold the recall pulse into a "Brain pulse" section: contradictions resolved overnight, top mentions, new facts since the last briefing, and a pending-consolidation footer
Summarize today's meetings with the participant context loaded below

sections.heading: GBrain-Native Context Loading
lines: Before generating any briefing, load context from gbrain systematically

sections.heading: Before a meeting
lines: For every attendee:
`gbrain search "{the attendee's name}"` -- find their brain page
`gbrain get {the attendee's slug}` -- load compiled truth, recent timeline, relationship context

sections.heading: Daily briefing queries
lines: `gbrain query "active deals status"` -- deal pipeline snapshot
`gbrain query "meetings this week"` -- recent meeting pages with insights
`gbrain query "pending commitments follow-ups"` -- open threads and action items
`gbrain search --type person --sort updated --limit 10` -- people in play

sections.heading: Before an email reply
lines: Before drafting or triaging any email, load sender context from the brain and
read their compiled truth. This turns a cold reply into an informed one

sections.heading: Freshness guard
lines: bind pages = invoke list pages with filter = "today"
let mine be the pages written by the input
if any urgent pages or any unwritten pages,
emit briefing.attention with status = "needs review"
bind briefing body =

codeblock:plain:REFJTFkgQlJJRUZJTkcgLS0gW2RhdGVdCj09PT09PT09PT09PT09PT09PT09PT09PQpbZm9yIGVhY2ggcGFnZSBpbiBtaW5lXS0ge3sgcGFnZS5pZCB9fQpbZW5kIGZvcl0KW2lmIGFueSB1cmdlbnQgcGFnZXNdTmVlZHMgYXR0ZW50aW9uW290aGVyd2lzZV1ObyB1cmdlbnQgcGFnZXNbZW5kIGlmXQ==
emit briefing.report with body = briefing body

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: codeblock:plain:

sections.heading: Back-Linking During Briefing
lines: use judgment to follow the Back-Linking During Briefing guidance:
If the briefing creates or updates any brain pages (e.g., new meeting prep
pages, updated entity pages), the back-linking iron law applies: every entity
the page mentions must have a back-link from their page. See
`skills/_brain-filing-rules.md`

sections.heading: Enforce back-links
lines: let mentioned be the entities mentioned by the input
for each entity in mentioned:
if the entity does not link to the input, add a back-link from the entity to the input

sections.heading: Citation in Briefings
lines: When presenting facts from brain pages, include inline citations:
"Jane is CTO of Acme [Source: people/jane-doe, updated 2026-04-01]"
This lets the user trace any claim back to the brain page and assess freshness

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Tools Used
lines: Search gbrain by name (query)
Read a page from gbrain (get_page)
List pages in gbrain by type (list_pages)
Check gbrain health (get_health)
View timeline entries in gbrain (get_timeline)
